foundation.title: CLASS
foundation.subtitle: Modern Records
foundation.content: A C++ Class can hold multiple variables and methods in one single object description.
foundation.scenario: A Student ID Card. It holds a Photo (Binary), a Name (Text), and a Number (Integer). One card is one person (Object).

examples.title: The Template
examples.explanation: Defining a new class structure using the 'class' keyword.
examples.code: class Player { int hp; void move(); };

examples.title: Visibility
examples.explanation: Controlling which parts of the program can see your variables with 'public' or 'private'.

examples.title: The Instance
examples.explanation: Creating a new object directly on the stack easily.

foundation.keyPoint: Classes keep data and actions together.

foundation.title: METHOD
foundation.subtitle: Object Skills
foundation.content: Method are functions that belong inside a class. They define what an object can 'DO'.
foundation.scenario: A Robot. The 'Walk' method is a skill the robot has (Action). You call the robot to use its skill (Call).

examples.title: Definition
examples.explanation: Writing the logic inside the class body.

examples.title: The Scope
examples.explanation: Using '::' to define a method outside the class blueprint.

examples.title: The Call
examples.explanation: Running the code inside the object capsule using its name and a dot ('.').

foundation.keyPoint: Methods have direct access to their own object's data.

foundation.title: VIRTUAL
foundation.subtitle: Polymorphism
foundation.content: A Virtual function allows you to redefine a skill in a child class while still seeing the same skill through the parent.
foundation.scenario: A Universal Speaker. You have a 'Speak' button. If you give the speaker a French Person, they speak French. If you give it a English Person, they speak English.

examples.title: The Super
examples.explanation: Marking a function as 'virtual' in the parent blueprint.

examples.title: The Override
examples.explanation: Using 'override' in the child class to change how the skill behaves.

examples.title: The Link
examples.explanation: Using a parent's pointer to hold any of its children objects dynamically.

foundation.keyPoint: virtual keyword enables true dynamic behavior.

gameData.hint: Unlike struct, it's safe by default.
